postText:
Most organisations treating AI adoption as a training problem will waste the next 18 months. And they will do it very confidently.

Anthropic just announced Claude Corps. Experts embedded directly into organisations to accelerate AI deployment. On paper, it sounds like the answer. It is the kind of thing that gets a standing ovation at the all-hands.

Here is what I have watched happen inside a telecoms company with 28,000 employees. The knowledge lands. The pilots run. The expert gets a lovely send-off. Then the organisation snaps back to exactly where it started, because the constraint was never understanding. It was accountability. Nobody owns what happens after the workshop. The workshop was the product.

A facilities management firm and a logistics company will give you completely different explanations for why AI stalled. Different jargon, different excuses, different PowerPoints. Same empty car park where the momentum used to be.

Embedded expertise compounds only if someone internal is accountable for carrying it forward. That person needs authority, not just a Slack channel and good intentions.

Claude Corps could work. It depends entirely on what it leaves behind, not what it delivers during the engagement.

What does your organisation actually do with external expertise once it walks out the door?

#EnterpriseAI #TechLeadership #DigitalTransformation
